narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a punch to the gut, especially when you've poured so much into getting here. Our prediction model looked at your profile and estimated a 44% chance you'd struggle to pass the bar exam down the line, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, placing you at the bottom of your class academically during that critical phase, and it was the strongest signal pulling the prediction toward failure. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 42.0, which fell short of the benchmarks that typically light the path to bar success. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 echoed that early challenge, reinforcing a pattern the model couldn't ignore. Coming from a middle-family income bracket added another layer of concern, as it often correlates with fewer resources for the intense prep needed. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.2, while respectable, wasn't enough to offset those hurdles in the model's view. Factors like being a white male pursuing full-time studies played smaller roles, but together they painted a picture of higher risk. It's not that you're incapable—far from it—but this specific combination has tripped up others before you, and we want to set everyone up for real wins. If you boost those academic deciles or retake the LSAT, you'd shift this story entirely. Hang in there; this is just one chapter, and you've got the grit to rewrite it.
